title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe (Bo Lo Bao) - Authentic & Easy
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get tips for a crispy topping and soft, fluffy bun. Perfect for Chinese baking!
seo.h1: Authentic Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?
How to Make Pineapple Buns: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for Perfect Pineapple Buns
Serving Suggestions for Bo Lo Bao
seo.imageAlt: A freshly baked golden-brown Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) with a crispy, sweet topping, ready to be enjoyed.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the sweet, fluffy goodness of homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao), a classic Chinese bakery treat perfect for breakfast or a snack!

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved Hong Kong bakery classic featuring a sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Beneath its golden, crispy crust lies a soft, fluffy milk bread bun, offering a delightful contrast of textures. Enjoy this iconic treat fresh from the oven, perfect for breakfast or an afternoon snack.

ingredients.notes: for bun dough, plus 1 for egg wash

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 2 cups all-purpose flour, 1/4 cup granulated sugar, 1 1/2 tsp instant yeast, and 1/2 tsp salt. Add 1/2 cup warm milk and 1 large egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for about 5 minutes, then incorporate 3 tbsp softened unsalted butter. Continue kneading for another 10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare the Pineapple Crust
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, prepare the crust. In a medium bowl, cream together 1/2 cup softened unsalted butter and 1/2 cup powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 egg yolk and 2 tbsp milk powder until well combined. Gradually add 1 cup all-purpose flour and 1/4 tsp baking powder, mixing until a soft dough forms. Wrap the crust dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the bun dough has doubled, gently punch it down and divide it into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rise for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffed.

instructions.title: Apply the Pineapple Crust
instructions.text: Take the chilled crust dough and divide it into 8 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface or between two sheets of parchment paper, roll out each portion into a thin, round disc large enough to cover the top of a bun. Gently place a crust disc over each risen bun, pressing lightly to adhere it to the top.

instructions.title: Egg Wash and Scoring
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 375F (190C). In a small bowl, whisk the remaining egg (from ingredient list, for egg wash) with a splash of water or milk. Brush the top of each crusted bun with a thin layer of egg wash. Using a sharp knife or a pastry scraper, carefully score a criss-cross pattern onto the crust, mimicking a pineapple's skin. Be careful not to cut too deep into the bun.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Bake the pineapple buns in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. If the crust browns too quickly, you may loosely tent the buns with aluminum foil for the last few minutes of baking. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly on a wire rack before serving.

tips: For an extra fluffy bun, ensure your milk is warm (not hot) to activate the yeast properly. Aim for around 105-115F (40-46C).
To achieve the signature crispy crust, make sure your butter for the crust is softened but not melted. Overworking the crust dough can make it tough.

Pineapple buns are best enjoyed fresh and warm from the oven. If storing, keep them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat gently in the oven for a few minutes to restore crispness.
article:
## Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao): A Sweet Taste of Chinese Baking History

There are few pastries as iconic and comforting in the world of Chinese baking as the Pineapple Bun, known in Cantonese as Bo Lo Bao. Despite its misleading name, this beloved bun contains no actual pineapple. Instead, its distinctive golden, crumbly topping, which cracks beautifully during baking, resembles the textured skin of a pineapple. Beneath this sweet, crunchy crust lies a soft, fluffy, slightly sweet bread that melts in your mouth.

Originating from Hong Kong, Bo Lo Bao has become a staple in bakeries, dim sum restaurants, and cha chaan tengs (Hong Kong-style cafes) across the globe. It’s a versatile treat, perfect for breakfast with a cup of milk tea, an afternoon snack, or a delightful dessert. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that magic at home, you're in luck, as we'll guide you through a fantastic Pineapple Bun Recipe, allowing you to bring this piece of culinary history right into your kitchen.

## What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?

The Pineapple Bun is a quintessential example of Hong Kong's unique fusion cuisine, blending Western baking techniques with local tastes. At its core, it's a sweet bread roll, but what truly sets it apart is its signature "pineapple" crust. This crust is a crumbly, cookie-like dough made primarily from sugar, flour, butter, and egg. When baked, it expands and cracks, forming a distinct pattern that gives the bun its name.

The contrast in textures is what makes Bo Lo Bao so addictive: the rich, buttery crunch of the topping against the airy, tender chewiness of the bread below. Traditionally, they are served warm, often with a generous slab of butter tucked inside, creating the famous "Bo Lo Yau" (pineapple bun with butter). It’s more than just a pastry; it's a cultural symbol, evoking nostalgia for many and offering a delightful experience for anyone who tries it.

## Ingredients for Homemade Pineapple Buns

Gathering the right components is the first step to a successful Pineapple Bun Recipe. Quality ingredients make a noticeable difference in the final product.

**For the Bread Dough:**
*   **All-Purpose Flour:** Provides the structure.
*   **Granulated Sugar:** Sweetens the dough and aids in browning.
*   **Instant Yeast:** The leavening agent that makes the bread rise.
*   **Salt:** Enhances flavor and controls yeast activity.
*   **Milk:** Adds richness and moisture. Whole milk is recommended.
*   **Egg:** Contributes to richness, color, and structure.
*   **Unsalted Butter (softened):** Adds tenderness, flavor, and moisture.

**For the Pineapple Topping:**
*   **Unsalted Butter (softened):** The base for the rich, crumbly texture.
*   **Powdered Sugar (or Granulated Sugar):** Sweetens the topping and contributes to its crispness.
*   **Egg Yolk:** Adds richness and helps bind the topping ingredients.
*   **All-Purpose Flour:** Provides structure for the crumbly topping.
*   **Milk Powder (optional):** Enhances flavor and browning. Custard powder can also be used for a more distinct yellow hue and flavor.
*   **Baking Powder (optional):** Helps the topping spread and crack.

**For the Egg Wash:**
*   **Egg Yolk:** For a golden, shiny finish.
*   **Milk or Water:** To thin the egg wash slightly.

## How to Make Pineapple Buns: Step-by-Step

Now, let's dive into the heart of our culinary adventure: the step-by-step Pineapple Bun Recipe. Patience is key, but the results are incredibly rewarding.

**Step 1: Prepare the Bread Dough**
1.  In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Whisk to combine.
2.  In a separate bowl, whisk together warm milk and egg.
3.  Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
4.  Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 5-7 minutes until it becomes somewhat smooth.
5.  Gradually add the softened butter, kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test (stretches thin enough to see light through without tearing). This may take another 8-10 minutes.
6.  Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

**Step 2: Prepare the Pineapple Topping**
1.  While the dough is rising, cream together the softened butter and powdered sugar in a medium bowl until light and fluffy.
2.  Beat in the egg yolk until fully combined.
3.  Sift in the flour (and milk powder/baking powder, if using) and mix until just combined, forming a soft, cohesive dough. Do not overmix.
4.  Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

**Step 3: Shape the Buns**
1.  Once the bread dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air.
2.  Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces (about 60-70g each). Roll each piece into a smooth ball.
3.  Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.

**Step 4: Apply the Topping**
1.  Divide the chilled pineapple topping dough into the same number of pieces as your bread dough.
2.  Take one piece of topping dough and flatten it into a disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter) using your hands or a rolling pin. It should be thin but not so thin that it tears easily.
3.  Carefully place a flattened topping disc over each bread dough ball, gently molding it to cover about two-thirds to three-quarters of the bun, leaving some exposed bread dough at the bottom.
4.  If desired, use a dough scraper or knife to gently score a crosshatch pattern on the surface of the topping. This helps create the classic "pineapple" appearance as it bakes.

**Step 5: Second Rise (Proofing)**
1.  Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel.
2.  Let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy (they won't double in size like the first rise, but they should feel light).

**Step 6: Bake**
1.  Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
2.  Just before baking, gently brush the tops of the buns (specifically the topping) with the egg wash.
3.  Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the bread is cooked through.
4.  Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly.

## Pro Tips for Perfect Pineapple Buns

Mastering the techniques in this Pineapple Bun Recipe will elevate your baking from good to extraordinary.

*   **Don't Rush the Proofing:** Proper rising time is crucial for fluffy bread. A warm, slightly humid environment (like an oven with the light on, or a proofing box) works wonders.
*   **Butter Temperature is Key:** Ensure both the bread dough butter and the topping butter are softened but not melted. This affects texture and workability.
*   **Handle the Topping Gently:** Overworking the topping dough can make it tough. Mix until just combined and chill it well so it's easier to handle and doesn't stick.
*   **Scoring for Aesthetics:** The crosshatch pattern isn't just for looks; it encourages the topping to crack beautifully, enhancing the "pineapple" effect.
*   **Even Baking:** Rotate your baking sheet halfway through baking for uniform browning.
*   **The Butter Slice:** For an authentic Hong Kong experience, slice open a warm pineapple bun and insert a thick slab of cold butter (Bo Lo Yau). The butter melts into the warm bread, creating a heavenly richness.

## Serving Suggestions for Bo Lo Bao

While delicious on its own, a warm Bo Lo Bao can be elevated with various accompaniments:

*   **Classic Bo Lo Yau:** As mentioned, a thick slice of cold butter inserted into a freshly baked, still-warm bun is the ultimate indulgence.
*   **With Coffee or Milk Tea:** This is the quintessential Hong Kong breakfast or afternoon tea pairing.
*   **Plain and Warm:** Sometimes, simplicity is best. Enjoy the pure flavors and textures straight from the oven.
*   **With Condensed Milk:** A drizzle of condensed milk adds extra sweetness and creamy texture.
*   **As a Snack:** Perfect for satisfying a sweet craving any time of day.

## Pineapple Bun Variations & FAQs

The classic Pineapple Bun is fantastic, but variations abound, and you might have some questions.

**Variations:**
*   **Bo Lo Char Siu Bao:** A savory twist, where the fluffy bread is filled with delicious barbecued pork before being topped with the pineapple crust.
*   **Custard-Filled Pineapple Buns:** A creamy, sweet egg custard filling adds another layer of indulgence.
*   **Coconut Filling:** Shredded coconut mixed with sugar and butter provides a tropical, chewy center.
*   **Mini Pineapple Buns:** Smaller versions are perfect for parties or as bite-sized treats.

**FAQs:**

*   **Why is it called Pineapple Bun if there's no pineapple?**
    It's named purely for its appearance. The golden, cracked, crumbly topping resembles the rough skin of a pineapple, not because it contains any pineapple fruit or flavor.

*   **Can I make the dough ahead of time?**
    Yes, you can prepare the bread dough and let it have its first rise in the refrigerator overnight. Punch it down in the morning, let it come to room temperature for about 30 minutes, then proceed with shaping and the second rise.

*   **How do I store leftover Pineapple Buns?**
    Store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1-2 days. For longer storage, freeze them in an airtight bag or container for up to a month. Reheat in a toaster oven or microwave for best results.

*   **Is it hard to make?**
    While it involves a few steps, following a detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe makes it very manageable for home bakers. The key is to be patient with the proofing and careful with the topping application. Don't be intimidated; the process is rewarding!

Armed with this comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe, you're ready to embark on your own Chinese baking journey. The aroma of freshly baked Bo Lo Bao filling your kitchen is an experience in itself, and the taste is a delightful reward. Don't hesitate to try this beloved Pineapple Bun Recipe and bring the taste of Hong Kong to your kitchen. Happy baking!
